text.title: Performance Testing Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Performance Testing methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Every performance test plan MUST declare numeric SLO targets (p50, p95, p99, RPS, error-rate ceiling) signed off by a named owner before any test run.

rationale.source: Site Reliability Engineering / Google SRE workbook

rule.rationale: Measuring without a target produces data without pass/fail; SLO encodes business risk tolerance and cannot be set unilaterally by an agent.

rule.statement: A baseline MUST exist at `.perf/baseline.json` committed to git with git SHA, environment, and dataset size before any optimisation work begins.
rule.rationale: Without a committed baseline, regressions and improvements are undetectable; metadata pins what was measured.

rule.statement: Load tests MUST use coordinated-omission-corrected tools (k6, wrk2, vegeta); Apache Bench and naive request-then-wait scripts are forbidden for tail-latency claims.
rule.rationale: Non-corrected tools synchronise request rate to server speed, hiding queuing delay; real clients do not wait politely.

rule.statement: Tests MUST run on staging with production-equivalent data volume; local-machine or empty-DB runs are rejected as evidence.
rule.rationale: Empty DBs produce optimistic query plans; local machines have different CPU governor / network / I/O than prod.

rule.statement: First 60 seconds of every run (cache warmup + JIT) MUST be discarded from recorded metrics.
rule.rationale: Cold-start data includes import cost and JIT compilation - not representative of steady state.

rule.statement: Plan MUST declare which of the 5 test types (load, stress, spike, endurance, scalability) is being run; multi-type runs are split into separate verdicts.
rule.rationale: Each type has different pass criteria; mixed plans produce ambiguous verdicts.

rule.statement: CI MUST run `perf-gate.py` comparing current run to baseline; exit 1 on regression beyond SLO budget.
rule.rationale: Manual diff inspection misses regressions; automated gate catches them at PR time.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
